mechanic.description: Each player may use the once-per-game Free Swap to immediately swap one of their own pieces on the board with any empty square (keeping turn order otherwise unchanged).
mechanic.python_code:
import numpy as np

def resource_once_per_game_free_swap(game_state: dict) -> dict:
    # Expected game_state keys: 'board', 'current_player', 'turn'
    board = game_state.get('board')
    if board is None:
        return game_state

    # Standardize board to numpy array if needed
    if not isinstance(board, np.ndarray):
        board = np.array(board, dtype='<U1')

    current_player = int(game_state.get('current_player', 1))
    player_char = 'X' if current_player == 1 else 'O'

    # Initialize per-player resource availability on first use
    if 'resource_free_swap_available' not in game_state:
        game_state['resource_free_swap_available'] = {1: True, 2: True}

    if not game_state['resource_free_swap_available'].get(current_player, False):
        return game_state

    # Trigger condition: if there exists at least one of the player's pieces
    # and at least one empty square, we perform the swap deterministically
    # to keep this mechanic automatic and self-contained.
    empties = np.argwhere(board == '_')
    own_cells = np.argwhere(board == player_char)
    if empties.size == 0 or own_cells.size == 0:
        return game_state

    # Choose a swap that tends to be helpful: move the piece that is
    # closest (Manhattan) to any empty square, and place into the first
    # empty cell in reading order.
    # This is deterministic and affects only one move/square placement.
    target_empty = empties[np.lexsort((empties[:, 1], empties[:, 0]))][0]

    # Compute Manhattan distance to the chosen empty
    ex, ey = int(target_empty[0]), int(target_empty[1])
    dists = np.abs(own_cells[:, 0] - ex) + np.abs(own_cells[:, 1] - ey)
    best_idx = int(np.argmin(dists))
    from_cell = own_cells[best_idx]
    fx, fy = int(from_cell[0]), int(from_cell[1])

    # Apply swap: set origin to blank, destination to player's piece
    board[fx, fy] = '_'
    board[ex, ey] = player_char

    # Mark resource as used
    game_state['resource_free_swap_available'][current_player] = False
    game_state['board'] = board
    return game_state
mechanic.justification: This adds a meaningful planning option to escape bad placements or set up a 4-in-a-row without creating constant board disruption.

parent_summary: Baseline game without the new mechanic.
